Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint For Damages
Off Calendar Mar-18-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS BIANA ROYKH, D.D.S., KAI LEI, JOHN, PHD, MSC FEATHERSTONE, MARK, DDS KIRKLAND, THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint For Damages is granted as to the punitive damages allegations and denied as to the new causes of action and newly added defendants. Mr. Stefan acknowledges that the motion should be granted as to his punitive damages allegations. Since Mr. Stefan is representing himself and there is no prejudice to defendants by Mr. Stefan having added claims and new defendants without prior permission from the court, the court will deem that Mr. Stefan received permission to have properly alleged new claims and added new defendants. In light of the ruling on defendants' demurrer, no useful purpose is served by requiring Mr. Stefan to file a motion for leave to allege new claims and add new defendants. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-18-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS KAI LEI, JOHN, PHD, MSC FEATHERSTONE, MARK, DDS KIRKLAND, THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, SAN FRANCISCO, BIANA ROYKH, D.D.S.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the tenth cause of action for malpractice and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to all other causes of action challenged by the demurrer. Mr. Stefan acknowledges that he should not allege a claim for malpractice. Mr. Stefan's request for leave to amend is granted in its entirety. Provided that he has a good faith basis to do so, Mr. Stefan is granted leave to: 1) correct the deficiencies in the causes of action challenged by the demurrer and any other causes of action he believes need to be corrected; 2) re-allege causes of action that he alleged in the first amended complaint but did not include in the second amended complaint; 3) allege new causes of action not previously alleged in any prior complaint; and 4) add additional defendants not previously named in the first amended complaint. Mr. Stefan should consider the granting of leave to amend as his final opportunity to properly allege all of the claims he believes he has a legal and factual basis to make and to name all the defendants that he believes are liable on those claims. The broad grant of leave to amend is supported by the strong policy of favoring resolution of disputes on their merits. However, it is now time for Mr. Stefan to finalize the claims he is making so that this case may proceed beyond the pleadings stage. ORDER SIGNED.
